title: The Divorce

jokes.text: Larry putting his drink down and leaving a ring stain on someone's nice table
jokes.setup: Larry casually puts his drink down on what appears to be nice furniture
jokes.punchline: The host's horrified reaction to the ring stain reveals Larry's social obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David setup - his casual disregard for social norms creates immediate conflict. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: Do you respect wood?
jokes.setup: Host is upset about the ring stain on their wooden table
jokes.punchline: The philosophical question about 'respecting wood' elevates a minor domestic issue to absurd heights
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the show's ability to turn mundane conflicts into philosophical absurdity. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Larry immediately calling Julia despite Cheryl explicitly saying no multiple times
jokes.setup: Cheryl repeatedly refuses to call Julia about the ring stain
jokes.punchline: Larry calls anyway the moment she steps away, showing complete disregard for her wishes
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Larry's inability to respect boundaries, leading directly to escalating conflict.

jokes.text: The Dutch apple pie divorce analogy where Larry prefers the crumbly topping to the filling
jokes.setup: Berg explains the divorce settlement using a Dutch apple pie metaphor
jokes.punchline: Larry completely misses the point and fixates on preferring the crumbly topping to the actual pie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the lawyer's metaphor - Larry's literal interpretation undermines the entire legal strategy.

jokes.text: It'd be a good desk to get blowjobs under. That's not why I bought it, but let's call it a happy accident.
jokes.setup: Larry comments on Berg's spacious desk
jokes.punchline: Berg's casual admission about the desk's sexual utility, delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Sharp, unexpected line with perfect deadpan delivery. The 'happy accident' phrasing elevates it.

jokes.text: You hired a bald man, huh? You should've recused yourself. We can't go up against each other. It's a code. It's in the bald code.
jokes.setup: Larry meets Cheryl's bald divorce attorney
jokes.punchline: Larry invents a fictional 'bald code' that prevents bald men from opposing each other
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry absurdity - creating elaborate social rules around arbitrary physical characteristics. Highly quotable concept.

jokes.text: And I can call you bald. It's like black people calling each other nigga.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the 'bald code' privileges
jokes.punchline: Larry makes an inappropriate racial analogy that immediately makes everyone uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Characteristic Larry boundary-crossing, but relies on shock value rather than clever construction.

jokes.text: Larry practicing a fake tic for dating to see if women will point it out
jokes.setup: Larry is doing some kind of repetitive motion
jokes.punchline: He reveals it's a fake tic he's testing as a dating strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly bizarre Larry logic - turning a neurological condition into a social experiment shows his warped psychology perfectly.

jokes.text: Jeff's friendly divorce proposal vs Larry's vicious response
jokes.setup: Jeff suggesting they'd have an amicable 50-50 divorce
jokes.punchline: Larry's extremely hostile response about taking Jeff's balls
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between Jeff's naive optimism and Larry's vindictive nature

jokes.text: I'm taking your balls and I'm thumbtacking them to the wall. You're gonna get nothing out of it.
jokes.setup: Jeff suggests they would have a friendly 50-50 divorce
jokes.punchline: Susie's graphic threat about what she'd actually do in a divorce
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie character moment - her violent imagery and intensity completely demolish Jeff's naive optimism.

jokes.text: I'll be slamming the door in her face. I hope that doesn't bother you.
jokes.setup: Joe warns that his 13-year-old daughter will be selling Girl Scout cookies
jokes.punchline: Larry matter-of-factly announces he'll slam the door on a child
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - completely inappropriate response delivered with casual honesty. Strong character consistency.

jokes.text: The buffet sharing dispute and Hiriam Katz's legal intervention
jokes.setup: Larry shares food from his buffet plate with friends who ordered off the menu
jokes.punchline: A lawyer happens to be dining there and launches into a full legal defense of Larry's right to share his food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of a minor restaurant dispute into full legal theater. The absurdity of proving 'prior intent' for buffet sharing is perfect.

jokes.text: There's no plus one here. You've never gotten a hookup!
jokes.setup: Jeff assumes he'll get to go to the Dodgers game with Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry brutally shuts down Jeff's assumption and denies he's ever provided hookups
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Larry's selfishness vs Jeff's expectation of friendship benefits.

jokes.text: I find them abhorrent. But come in.
jokes.setup: Young Kyra asks if Larry likes Girl Scout cookies
jokes.punchline: Larry gives a brutally honest negative answer but immediately invites her in anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - brutal honesty immediately undercut by social courtesy. 'Abhorrent' is an absurdly strong word for cookies.

jokes.text: Oh, thank you berry munch, ooooh. Th-th-th-thank you berry munch.
jokes.setup: Kyra naming cookie flavors
jokes.punchline: Larry mocking the cookie name with exaggerated pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Minor wordplay moment, not particularly strong

jokes.text: I think I just had my first period.
jokes.setup: Larry is enthusiastically talking about coconut cookies
jokes.punchline: Kyra drops this bombshell, creating maximum awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Maximum cringe setup - puts Larry in the most uncomfortable possible situation. The timing is perfect.

jokes.text: I, uh, think I just had my first period.
jokes.setup: Normal girl scout cookie transaction
jokes.punchline: Sudden awkward medical situation
jokes.explanation: Major plot turn creating massive awkwardness

jokes.text: Larry's reaction beat to the period announcement
jokes.setup: Kyra announces her first period
jokes.punchline: Larry's stunned silence and processing of this information
jokes.explanation: Strong reaction beat - Larry's processing of this nightmare scenario creates comedy through silence.

jokes.text: The entire tampon instruction sequence
jokes.setup: Larry tries to help by reading tampon instructions to a 13-year-old
jokes.punchline: The increasingly graphic and confused instruction session
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - Larry trapped in the most inappropriate situation possible, trying to be helpful but making it worse.

jokes.text: What is a Jew doing on a motorcycle? I never recall ever seeing a Jew on a motorcycle.
jokes.setup: Larry saw his lawyer Berg on a motorcycle
jokes.punchline: Larry's ethnic stereotyping about Jews and motorcycles
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry ethnic observation - mildly amusing but not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: It's 'fro Larry. 'Fro Larry on the motor scooter.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Larry's college motor scooter accident
jokes.punchline: The image of young Larry with his big afro on a tiny motor scooter
jokes.explanation: Amusing visual callback to Larry's younger self - the contrast works well.

jokes.text: If her nose is running and she needed a tissue, I would have given her a tissue. Same thing.
jokes.setup: Larry defending helping Kyra with her period
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely inappropriate comparison of tampons to tissues
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - his complete inability to understand why the situation was inappropriate. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm too lazy [to get divorced].
jokes.setup: Larry asks why Marty doesn't just get divorced
jokes.punchline: Marty's brutally honest admission about staying married out of laziness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan honesty - reduces marriage to pure inertia. Perfect economy and character truth.

jokes.text: The housekeeper refusing Larry entry with detailed explanations about interest vs. tickets
jokes.setup: Larry tries to visit O'Donnell but the housekeeper won't let him in
jokes.punchline: The housekeeper's elaborate philosophical distinction between being 'interested' and having actual tickets
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - the housekeeper's logic about interest vs. tickets is absurdly precise and destroys Larry's assumptions.

jokes.text: Mr. O'Donnell say you did bad thing to miss Kyra. A bad thing? Yes. Oh no no no no no. I didn't do anything bad to miss Kyra.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to see O'Donnell
jokes.punchline: Housekeeper's broken English making tampon help sound sinister
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of tampon consequences

jokes.text: To me, it's kind of like public school. You went to school, you got through it and now you move on.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl discussing how they view their marriage after divorce
jokes.punchline: Larry compares marriage to public school - something you endure and move past
jokes.explanation: Sharp analogy that perfectly captures Larry's unsentimental view of relationships. The comparison is both funny and revealing.

jokes.text: Do you think there's any chance we could have sex? What's the difference? When there are women in this bedroom, it seems crazy if I don't at least offer.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl having a pleasant conversation during the divorce process
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely inappropriate proposition and bizarre justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - his transactional view of sex and complete misreading of social cues.

jokes.text: Do you think there's any chance we could have sex? Um, no. Come on. What's the difference? Honestly, think about it, what's the difference? Let's just do it. Come on.
jokes.setup: Finalizing divorce
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate sexual proposition with flawed logic
jokes.explanation: Classically inappropriate Larry request

jokes.text: When there are women in this bedroom, it seems crazy if I don't at least offer.
jokes.setup: Cheryl rejecting sex
jokes.punchline: Larry's twisted logic about bedroom etiquette
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly warped Larry social logic

jokes.text: First of all, I commend you on the demographics.
jokes.setup: A diverse group of Girl Scouts comes to collect cookie money
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate comment about their racial diversity
jokes.explanation: Characteristic Larry inappropriate observation - his treating diversity like a business strategy.

jokes.text: Larry complimenting girl scouts' demographics
jokes.setup: Girl scouts arriving for cookie payment
jokes.punchline: Larry treating diversity like a conscious casting choice
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but characteristic Larry observation about race and ethnicity

jokes.text: Oh, well, they don't. I mean, why would they? I'm not Jewish.
jokes.setup: Larry assumes Berg is Jewish and went to a Catholic school as an exception
jokes.punchline: Berg casually reveals he's not Jewish at all
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection payoff - completely reframes all of Berg's previous behavior and Larry's assumptions.

jokes.text: Berg revealing he's not Jewish
jokes.setup: Larry assuming Berg is Jewish based on name and behavior
jokes.punchline: Berg casually revealing he's not Jewish at all
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that recontextualizes earlier behavior, good setup payoff

jokes.text: Well, you're Jewish. It's only respectful.
jokes.setup: Larry questions why Berg said 'Mazel tov' if he's not Jewish
jokes.punchline: Berg's reasonable explanation that makes Larry look paranoid
jokes.explanation: Good callback that recontextualizes Berg's behavior as politeness rather than deception.

jokes.text: Well, you're Jewish. It's only respectful. On the high holy days, I said, 'hey, you going to temple, Berg?' And you went, 'no.' Right, 'cause I'm not Jewish so why would I go to temple? Right, like, 'I'm a Jew, but not a practicing Jew.'
jokes.setup: Berg explaining his respectful Jewish phrases
jokes.punchline: Larry's realization he was fooled by fake Jewish behavior
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing extent of the deception

jokes.text: I got a Swede lawyer? She's gonna get everything!
jokes.setup: Learning Berg is Swedish
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate panic about ethnic lawyer disadvantage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry ethnic paranoia delivered with panic

jokes.text: I got sweded.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes his Swedish lawyer has been deceiving him about being Jewish
jokes.punchline: Larry turns 'Swedish' into a verb meaning 'deceived'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Larry's sense of ethnic betrayal. Great economy and quotability.

jokes.text: You know what you do when you get a fucking divorce? Ride around and bump the horn and shit like motherfuckers getting married.
jokes.setup: Marty announces he's getting divorced
jokes.punchline: Leon's idea to celebrate divorce like a wedding with car horns and cans
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept - celebrating divorce with wedding traditions. Leon's enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Guess who's getting a divorce. What? Martin Norton Funkhouser. Really? And it's because of you!
jokes.setup: Funkhouser's earlier marriage complaints
jokes.punchline: Larry accidentally inspiring his divorce
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Larry's unintended influence

jokes.text: You ran that ass in the ground like a rent-a-car.
jokes.setup: Leon insisting that Larry 'ran that ass in the ground' in his marriage
jokes.punchline: The rent-a-car comparison for how men treat marriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon analogy - crude but insightful. The rent-a-car comparison is both funny and accurate.

jokes.text: You sure you're really Jewish? I'm the real deal.
jokes.setup: Larry meets with Hiriam Katz after being burned by the fake-Jewish Berg
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid verification of Katz's Jewish credentials
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Larry's newfound paranoia about ethnic authenticity.

jokes.text: O'Donnell lost the team in the divorce, so his wife owns it now
jokes.setup: Larry and Marty arrive at the stadium expecting to use O'Donnell's box
jokes.punchline: The revelation that O'Donnell lost everything in his divorce
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic twist - Larry's good deed backfires spectacularly. Great structure and earned payoff.

jokes.text: Oh my God! He's meeting with Cheryl's lawyers right now! They're gonna finalize the papers. I've got to get over there.
jokes.setup: Realizing Katz is bad for husbands
jokes.punchline: Larry's panic about his own divorce settlement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing consequences catching up

jokes.text: Don't worry. I think I've got something that will stop the bleeding. I think it's working. It's doing the job.
jokes.setup: Larry bleeding from punch
jokes.punchline: Using tampon to stop nosebleed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback - tampon knowledge finally useful

jokes.text: Larry using tampon to stop nosebleed
jokes.setup: Larry's bloody nose from the punch
jokes.punchline: Using the feminine product from earlier episode
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that gives unexpected payoff to earlier tampon scene. Great visual.

jokes.text: Larry using a tampon to stop his nosebleed
jokes.setup: Larry gets punched in the nose by O'Donnell
jokes.punchline: He uses a tampon from Cheryl's bathroom supplies to stop the bleeding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that gives the tampon incident unexpected practical value. Great visual and structural payoff.

jokes.text: You got the house so you have 24 hours to vacate the premises.
jokes.setup: Larry thinks he's getting a great divorce deal
jokes.punchline: Cheryl reveals he actually lost the house and has to leave immediately
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Larry's confidence in his lawyer completely backfires. Strong structural payoff.

jokes.text: Fucking two bitches in your bed. What? Fucking two bitches in your bed. You had sex with two women in my bedroom? Fucking right I did! How you gonna fuck bitches in a twin bed.
jokes.setup: Leon's memories of the house
jokes.punchline: Revelation of Leon's sexual activities in Larry's bed
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon inappropriate revelation

jokes.text: Fucking two bitches in your bed. How you gonna fuck bitches in a twin bed?
jokes.setup: Larry discovers Leon had sex in his bed
jokes.punchline: Leon's crude justification about bed size logistics
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon crudeness with practical logic. Character-consistent but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I don't even like girl scout cookies.
jokes.setup: The Girl Scouts return demanding payment for cookies Larry ordered
jokes.punchline: Larry's final declaration that he never liked the cookies anyway
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the opening cookie interaction, showing Larry's consistency in his disdain.

tmdb_overview: Larry learns his lawyer isn't kosher, and rescinds a cookie order from the Girl Scout daughter of a beleaguered sports owner.
